questions.q: Whose daughter was princess Uttara?
questions.explanation: Uttara was the daughter of King Virata, ruler of the Matsya kingdom.

questions.q: Who taught princess Uttara dance and music for a year?
options: A gandharva master from Indra's court
Brihannala, who was Arjuna in disguise
questions.explanation: Her teacher Brihannala was Arjuna, living out his thirteenth year of exile in disguise.

questions.q: Whom did Uttara marry?
questions.explanation: Uttara married Abhimanyu, the son of Arjuna and Subhadra.

questions.q: Who was Uttara's famous son?
questions.explanation: Uttara bore Parikshit, the sole heir who carried the Kuru line beyond the war.

questions.q: Who was Uttara's father-in-law?
questions.explanation: Her husband Abhimanyu was Arjuna's son, making Arjuna, her former dance teacher, her father-in-law.

questions.q: How did Uttara become a widow so young?
options: Abhimanyu was killed inside the Chakravyuha on the thirteenth day of the war
Abhimanyu fell to Karna's dart in single combat
Abhimanyu died in the night raid on the camp
Abhimanyu was poisoned at a feast in Hastinapura
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu was trapped and killed inside Drona's wheel formation on the thirteenth day of the Kurukshetra war.

questions.q: Who saved Uttara's unborn child from a celestial weapon?
questions.explanation: Krishna turned aside the Brahmastra aimed at her womb and later revived the stillborn child.

questions.q: Who aimed the Brahmastra at Uttara's womb?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama, unable to recall his weapon, bent it toward Uttara's unborn child to end the Pandava line.

questions.q: Which brother of princess Uttara rode against the Kauravas with Brihannala as his charioteer?
questions.explanation: Her brother prince Uttara rode out against the Kuru cattle raiders with the disguised Arjuna driving his chariot.

questions.q: Of which kingdom was Uttara a princess?
questions.explanation: Uttara was a princess of Matsya, the kingdom of her father Virata.

questions.q: Why did Arjuna decline to marry Uttara when Virata offered her hand?
options: As her teacher he had seen her as a daughter, so he took her as a daughter-in-law instead
He was bound by a vow of celibacy taken during the exile
Draupadi had forbidden him to take another wife
He had already promised her hand to Krishna's son
questions.explanation: Having lived beside her as her teacher, Arjuna accepted Uttara for his son Abhimanyu, which also proved his conduct toward her had been blameless.

questions.q: Where were Uttara and Abhimanyu married?
options: At Viratanagara, in the hall of dance
At Upaplavya, in the Matsya kingdom
At Dwarka, the city of the Yadavas
questions.explanation: The wedding was celebrated at Upaplavya, where the Pandavas had settled after the year of disguise ended.

questions.q: What did young Uttara ask Brihannala to bring back from the battle against the Kuru raiders?
options: Peacock plumes from the royal tents
The crown of a defeated king
A captured chariot with white horses
Fine garments of the warriors as dresses for her dolls
questions.explanation: She playfully asked for the rich cloths of the Kuru warriors for her dolls, and Arjuna gathered them from the stupefied host.

questions.q: By popular tradition, how old was Uttara's husband Abhimanyu when he fell at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Abhimanyu was only sixteen when he was slain in the Chakravyuha, leaving the young Uttara a widow.

questions.q: Who was Uttara's mother?
questions.explanation: Uttara was the daughter of Queen Sudeshna, the wife of King Virata.

questions.q: In what condition was Uttara's son born?
options: Healthy but blind, until sages healed him
Premature, and hidden away for a year
Marked on the chest by the weapon's fire
questions.explanation: Struck by the Brahmastra in the womb, Parikshit was delivered stillborn and brought to life by Krishna.

questions.q: Why is Uttara remembered as the last mother of the Kuru line?
options: She raised the orphans of the war as her own
Her child was the dynasty's only surviving heir
She was the final queen crowned in Hastinapura
All her co-wives died before the war ended
questions.explanation: With every other heir dead, the child in Uttara's womb was the single thread by which the Kuru dynasty survived.

questions.q: What relation was Krishna to Uttara's husband Abhimanyu?
options: His guru in the arts of war
His maternal uncle, brother of Subhadra
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu was the son of Krishna's sister Subhadra, so Krishna was his maternal uncle.

questions.q: How did Uttara's betrothal to Abhimanyu come about?
options: Virata offered her to Arjuna, who accepted her for his son instead
Krishna proposed the match to seal the war alliance
Abhimanyu won her at a swayamvara held in Matsya
Sudeshna arranged it secretly through Sairandhri
questions.explanation: When Virata offered his daughter to Arjuna in marriage, Arjuna took her as a bride for Abhimanyu, having been her teacher and guardian.

questions.q: By what other name was Uttara's brother, prince Uttara, known?
questions.explanation: The prince of Matsya was also called Bhuminjaya, conqueror of the earth.

questions.q: Which guests arrived with rich gifts for Uttara's wedding?
options: Krishna and Balarama with the Yadavas
Shalya and the princes of Madra
The heirs of Jarasandha from Magadha
questions.explanation: Krishna and Balarama came from Dwarka with Abhimanyu, Subhadra, and lavish wedding gifts for the celebration at Upaplavya.

questions.q: Who persuaded Brihannala to serve as prince Uttara's charioteer against the Kurus?
options: Kanka, with a quiet word in court
Princess Uttara herself, prompted by Sairandhri
questions.explanation: Sairandhri revealed that Brihannala had once driven Arjuna's chariot, and princess Uttara carried the request to her teacher.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata tradition, what did Uttara do as Ashwatthama's weapon blazed toward her?
options: She shielded her womb with Abhimanyu's shield
She prayed to Surya, ancestor of kings
She ran to Krishna, begging him to save her unborn child
She fainted, and Subhadra spoke for her
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana describes Uttara running to Krishna in terror, who then shielded the child in her womb with his own power.

questions.q: Why, in the epic's telling, was Uttara's son given the name Parikshit?
options: He was born when the Kuru line had all but perished
He was destined to examine the conduct of kings
Vyasa named him for the rite that revived him
He was born under the constellation of testing
questions.explanation: The name is linked to the line being parikshina, nearly extinguished, at his birth; some retellings also derive it from his being tested by the weapon.

questions.q: In which parva does Ashwatthama turn the Brahmastra upon Uttara's unborn child?
questions.explanation: The episode follows the night massacre in the Sauptika Parva, when Ashwatthama bends his weapon toward the wombs of the Pandava women.

questions.q: What was the fate of Uttara's brother, prince Uttara, in the great war?
options: He survived and ruled Matsya after Virata
He was slain by Bhishma while defending Shveta
He fell in the night raid of Ashwatthama
He was killed by Shalya on the very first day
questions.explanation: Prince Uttara attacked Shalya on the first day of battle and was killed by the Madra king's dart.

questions.q: Through Uttara's marriage, King Virata's house became kin to which family?
options: The Pandavas, through Arjuna's son Abhimanyu
The Madra line of King Shalya
questions.explanation: Her wedding to Abhimanyu made Virata kin to the Pandavas and committed Matsya's strength to their cause in the war.

questions.q: By what means did Krishna revive Uttara's stillborn son?
options: He invoked the Sudarshana to burn away the weapon's venom
He made a solemn declaration of his own unbroken truth and dharma
He transferred a year of his own life to the child
He summoned Dhanvantari, the physician of the gods
questions.explanation: Krishna performed an act of truth, declaring that as he had never forsaken truth and righteousness, the child should live, and Parikshit stirred.

questions.q: Why did everything depend on the child in Uttara's womb after the war?
options: Yudhishthira had renounced the fathering of heirs
The elders had cursed all other claimants
A prophecy named her son the only rightful king
Every other heir, including Draupadi's five sons, had been slain
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu, Ghatotkacha, and Draupadi's five sons were all dead, leaving Uttara's unborn child as the dynasty's only future.

questions.q: Who was Uttara's mother-in-law?
questions.explanation: Uttara's husband Abhimanyu was the son of Subhadra, Krishna's sister, who was therefore her mother-in-law.
